content_of_post_to_answer: What strategies can help me focus on completing study goals instead of obsessing over the number of hours studied?
answer: Obsession with hours usually appears when the outcome of studying feels invisible, so time becomes a stand-in for certainty. But hours measure exposure, not retention. The shift is to treat study as producing evidence of recall or problem-solving ability, not time spent. When you can see what you can do without notes, the clock stops being the unit of progress.
